name: Ubiquiti UniFi Switch Enterprise 8 PoE
title: Ubiquiti UniFi Switch Enterprise 8 PoE
title_extended: USW Enterprise 8 PoE, 8 RJ45 2.5GbE Ports, 2 SFP+ 10G Ports, 1.3'' touchscreen
short_description: USW Enterprise 8 PoE, 8 RJ45 2.5GbE Ports, 2 SFP+ 10G Ports, 1.3'' touchscreen
description: The UniFi Switch Enterprise 8 PoE is a compact Layer 3 switch designed for environments that require reliable multi-gigabit connectivity combined with integrated power delivery. It features eight 2.5 GbE RJ45 ports, all supporting PoE+, allowing the switch to deliver both data and power to connected devices such as access points, IP phones, and cameras over a single Ethernet cable. This simplifies deployment, reduces cabling complexity, and helps keep installations clean and efficient. In addition to copper ports, the switch includes two high-speed 10G SFP+ uplinks that enable fast connections to aggregation switches, servers, or network storage. This makes it well suited for scenarios where high-bandwidth backhaul is required, such as offices with multiple access points or creative environments handling large data transfers. The device is optimized for PoE+ output and provides a generous total PoE power budget, enabling multiple powered devices to operate simultaneously. It is important to note that while the switch supports PoE+, it is not designed for PoE++ use cases and therefore is not compatible with newer access points that require higher power levels. Overall, this switch offers a balanced combination of performance, flexibility, and simplicity for growing networks.

detailed_description.heading: Compact Switch for Modern Networks
detailed_description.text: The UniFi Switch Enterprise 8 PoE is a compact Layer 3 switch designed for environments that require reliable multi-gigabit connectivity combined with integrated power delivery. It features eight 2.5 GbE RJ45 ports, all supporting PoE+, allowing the switch to deliver both data and power to connected devices such as access points, IP phones, and cameras over a single Ethernet cable. This simplifies deployment, reduces cabling complexity, and helps keep installations clean and efficient. In addition to copper ports, the switch includes two high-speed 10G SFP+ uplinks that enable fast connections to aggregation switches, servers, or network storage. This makes it well suited for scenarios where high-bandwidth backhaul is required, such as offices with multiple access points or creative environments handling large data transfers. The device is optimized for PoE+ output and provides a generous total PoE power budget, enabling multiple powered devices to operate simultaneously. It is important to note that while the switch supports PoE+, it is not designed for PoE++ use cases and therefore is not compatible with newer access points that require higher power levels. Overall, this switch offers a balanced combination of performance, flexibility, and simplicity for growing networks.

detailed_description.heading: Layer 3 Performance
detailed_description.text: Built as a true Layer 3 switch, the UniFi Switch Enterprise 8 PoE provides routing capabilities that go beyond basic switching. It supports local DHCP services, inter-VLAN routing, and static routing, enabling efficient traffic segmentation and optimized data flows directly at the switch level. This reduces unnecessary load on gateways and improves overall network responsiveness. On the Layer 2 side, the switch includes a rich feature set designed for stable and secure operation in demanding environments. Link aggregation allows multiple physical connections to operate as a single logical link, increasing throughput and redundancy. Spanning Tree protocols help prevent network loops, while advanced multicast handling ensures efficient delivery of video and voice traffic. Security and control are integral to the platform. Features such as port authentication, access control lists, traffic isolation, and storm control help protect the network from unauthorized access and misconfigured devices. Quality-of-service mechanisms further ensure that latency-sensitive applications such as voice or real-time data are prioritized appropriately. Together, these capabilities make the switch suitable for professional networks that require both performance and granular control.

detailed_description.heading: Designed for Continuous Use
detailed_description.text: Despite its compact desktop form factor, the UniFi Switch Enterprise 8 PoE is engineered for continuous, dependable operation. It uses an internal power supply with a universal AC input, allowing it to be deployed globally without additional adapters. Power efficiency is carefully managed to maintain stable operation even when multiple PoE-powered devices are connected. The enclosure is constructed from durable polycarbonate, providing a balance of strength, low weight, and thermal stability. Passive cooling and optimized heat dissipation ensure quiet operation, making the switch suitable for offices, meeting rooms, and technical spaces where noise levels matter. Environmental resilience is another key advantage. The switch is designed to operate reliably across a wide range of temperatures and humidity levels, ensuring consistent performance in different deployment conditions. Integrated protection against electrostatic discharge further enhances durability during installation and maintenance. A built-in touchscreen display on the front panel provides immediate access to essential device information and status indicators, simplifying on-site diagnostics. Altogether, the combination of efficient power design, robust construction, and thoughtful hardware details ensures long-term reliability in daily operation.

detailed_description.heading: Centralized Management
detailed_description.text: The switch is fully managed through the UniFi Network application, providing a unified interface for configuration, monitoring, and ongoing maintenance. From a single dashboard, administrators can view port status, monitor traffic patterns, apply network policies, and deploy firmware updates across the entire UniFi ecosystem. This centralized approach significantly reduces operational complexity and enables consistent configuration across multiple sites. Remote access capabilities allow network administrators to manage the switch securely without being physically present, which is especially valuable for distributed deployments and managed service providers. Integration with the UniFi ecosystem ensures seamless interoperability with other UniFi devices, creating a cohesive and scalable network environment. Compliance and certification are also key considerations. The switch meets NDAA requirements and carries multiple international certifications, making it suitable for use in regulated industries, enterprise environments, and public sector projects. These certifications confirm adherence to safety, electromagnetic compatibility, and quality standards. By combining centralized management, ecosystem integration, and regulatory compliance, the UniFi Switch Enterprise 8 PoE delivers a professional networking solution tailored for modern, performance-driven infrastructures.
